methods.description: Field studies conducted to collect fresh specimens of nocturnal velvet ants using various trapping techniques.

steps.description: Collections were made of male and female nocturnal mutillids at 60 field sites across the southwestern United States.
steps.searchable_summary: collect nocturnal velvet ant specimens from field sites

steps.description: Specimens were collected using blacklight traps, fluorescent lantern traps, and by hand.
steps.searchable_summary: use blacklight and fluorescent lantern traps to collect specimens

steps.description: Those collected with light traps were captured in soapy water and were transferred into 95% ethanol, whereas all hand-collected specimens were placed directly into 95% ethanol.
All specimens were identified to the species level except for some female specimens that were sorted to morphospecies.

methods.description: Molecular techniques for DNA extraction, PCR, and sequencing to analyze genetic material of specimens.

steps.description: DNA extraction, polymerase chain reaction (PCR), and sequencing were performed following the protocol described by Pilgrim and Pitts (2006).
steps.searchable_summary: extract DNA and perform PCR and sequencing

steps.description: PCR took place in a 20-l volume with conditions of 3 mM MgCl2, 200 pM dNTPs, 2 U of Taq polymerase, 1 mM each primer, and standard PCR buffer concentration.
steps.searchable_summary: set up PCR reaction with specified conditions

steps.description: Amplified products were visualized on agarose gels stained with ethidium bromide.
steps.searchable_summary: visualize PCR products on agarose gel

steps.description: Successful PCR products were cleaned using isopropanol purification.

ITS1 and ITS2 were sequenced for representatives of each available species and sex, sequences were aligned, and females were associated with males based on identical or nearly identical DNA sequences.
steps.searchable_summary: sequence and align ITS1 and ITS2 regions

methods.description: Bayesian analysis and haplotype network construction to study genetic relationships and divergence.

steps.description: The two genetic loci were subjected to Bayesian analysis using MrBayes version 3.1.2.
Bayesian analyses included four independent runs with three heated chains and one cold chain in each run.
steps.searchable_summary: run Bayesian MCMC with specified chains

steps.description: A parsimony-based haplotype network was constructed using the combined ITS1 and ITS2 sequences for all Dilophotopsis specimens by using TCS version 1.21.

methods.description: Estimation of divergence dates using penalized likelihood and Bayesian MCMC approaches.

steps.description: Divergence date estimates were calculated using a penalized likelihood approach with r8s 1.71.
Divergence date estimates were calculated using a Bayesian MCMC averaging approach with BEAST version 1.4.8.
